persona.name: Meridian Bank
persona.ownership: Public (NYSE: MRDN), holding company Meridian Bancshares, Inc.
persona.hosting: Hybrid: FIS-hosted core banking (Profile platform), on-prem data center Charlotte NC, Azure AD for identity, AWS for digital channels (online/mobile banking)

compliance_state.ffiec_cat: Inherent risk profile = High, maturity profile = Intermittent across all five domains, full re-assessment annually with quarterly refresh
compliance_state.ffiec_it_examination_handbook: Last IT exam 2025-Q3, Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) closed 2026-Q1
compliance_state.nydfs_23_nyccr_500: Exempt (no NY operations), tracked as adjacent framework
compliance_state.sox_itgc: Active since IPO 2018, 2025 audit clean, 2026 cycle in progress
compliance_state.glba_safeguards_rule: Program current, 2026 revision incorporated, continuous risk assessment
compliance_state.pci_dss_4_0: ROC issued 2025-Q4 (Fiserv-scope card services), AOC current through 2026-Q4
compliance_state.soc1_type2: Issued annually for core banking services supporting correspondent bank customers
compliance_state.soc2_type2: Not currently pursued (banking regulators prefer FFIEC over SOC 2)

geographic_scope.data_residency: United States only, FIS-hosted core in Dallas TX and Birmingham AL
geographic_scope.remote_workers: 18% of workforce (call center, IT, corporate functions)

perimeters.name: FIS Profile Core Banking System
perimeters.description: Hosted core banking platform (FIS Profile) running deposit accounts, loans, general ledger. Operated as a managed service under FIS BAA-equivalent (vendor contract with security exhibits). Serves all 240 branches and digital channels.
in_scope: Retail deposit accounts (DDA, savings, MMA, CDs)
Customer master and CIF (Customer Information File)
out_of_scope: Wealth management trust platform (separate perimeter, Jack Henry)
Card services cardholder data environment (separate PCI perimeter, Fiserv)

perimeters.name: Wealth Management and Trust Operations
perimeters.description: Jack Henry SilverLake platform for trust operations, wealth management customer records, fiduciary accounts. Lower transaction volume than core but higher per-account value.

perimeters.name: Wire Transfer and Correspondent Banking
perimeters.description: ACI Worldwide Posttrade/Wire processing, FedLine Advantage for Federal Reserve access, correspondent bank interfaces (Pershing clearing). Highest-value per-transaction, primary SOC 1 audit scope.
in_scope: FedLine Advantage access (Federal Reserve Fedwire and FedACH)
out_of_scope: ACH originated through retail channels (handled by FIS Profile)

perimeters.name: Digital Banking Channels
perimeters.description: Customer-facing web and mobile banking, Zelle P2P, online bill pay, ATM jackpotting-resistant fleet. Built and operated in-house on AWS with Azure AD B2C for consumer identity.

frameworks_to_load.name: FFIEC Cybersecurity Assessment Tool (CAT)
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Primary federal cybersecurity framework, baseline against which OCC measures inherent risk and maturity

frameworks_to_load.name: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Reference handbook covering IT audit, business continuity, development/acquisition, infosec, management, operations, outsourcing, retail payments, wholesale payments

frameworks_to_load.name: NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 (Cybersecurity Requirements for Financial Services)
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Adjacent compliance: tracked even though Meridian has no NY operations, because correspondent banking relationships may pull Meridian into NY regulatory scope

frameworks_to_load.name: SOX ITGC (Sarbanes-Oxley IT General Controls)
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Public-company reporting controls, audited annually for material financial systems (FIS Profile, wire processing, card services)

frameworks_to_load.name: GLBA Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314)
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Customer information protection standard, mandatory information security program, board-approved annually

frameworks_to_load.name: FFIEC BCP/DR / Business Continuity Management Handbook
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Recovery time objectives and resilience testing standards for core banking, wires, card services

frameworks_to_load.name: PCI DSS 4.0
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Cardholder data environment (Fiserv card services), annual ROC, quarterly ASV scans, penetration testing

frameworks_to_load.name: NIST CSF 2.0 (cross-reference)
frameworks_to_load.purpose: Common taxonomy mapping against FFIEC CAT, supports vendor diligence questionnaires and OCC examiner dialogue

audit_scope.primary: FFIEC CAT (OCC annual exam) + SOX ITGC (PCAOB auditor annual)
audit_scope.secondary: GLBA Safeguards Rule + PCI DSS 4.0 ROC + NYDFS 23 NYCRR 500 adjacent

risk_register_seed.title: ATM jackpotting at regional branch fleet
Wire fraud via business email compromise (BEC) targeting commercial customers
ACH fraud (origination debiting or returns manipulation)
Synthetic identity lending losses (auto loan and unsecured consumer)
Correspondent banking AML/KYC gap (Pershing or downstream foreign bank)
Core banking vendor concentration on FIS Profile (single point of failure)
Ransomware on FIS-hosted core banking host (managed environment, shared responsibility)
Third-party core banking breach at FIS exposing customer NPI
Destructive malware / wiper attack on internal branch network
Insider trading via material non-public information (MNPI) accessed through trust operations
Critical vendor SLA breach during quarter-end close (FIS Profile or ACI)
Disaster recovery failure - inability to fail over from Charlotte primary data center during regional event

policy_set: Wire Transfer Authentication and Dual Control Policy
Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan (FFIEC BCP)
Incident Response Policy (with NYDFS 72-hour notification clock and GLBA multi-state breach notification)
Vendor Management and Due Diligence Policy
Anti-Money Laundering / Bank Secrecy Act (AML/BSA) Policy
OFAC Compliance and Sanctions Screening Policy
Records Retention Policy (12 categories, FFIEC and SOX retention schedules)
Cybersecurity Awareness and Training Policy (mandatory annual + role-based)

sample_vendors.name: FIS (Fidelity National Information Services)
sample_vendors.service: Core banking platform (Profile), deposit and loan servicing for 240 branches and digital channels
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: OCC-supervised service provider, FFIEC IT Examination Handbook Outsourcing Booklet

sample_vendors.name: Jack Henry & Associates
sample_vendors.service: Trust operations platform (SilverLake), wealth management customer records and fiduciary accounts

sample_vendors.name: Fiserv
sample_vendors.service: Card services issuer processor, debit and credit card transactions, ATM driving for Fiserv-network ATMs

sample_vendors.name: ACI Worldwide
sample_vendors.service: Wire transfer processing (Posttrade), AML transaction monitoring wires
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: SOX ITGC in-scope, wire room controls

sample_vendors.name: Federal Reserve FedLine Advantage
sample_vendors.service: Fedwire and FedACH access, primary federal settlement
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: Federal Reserve-supervised, no BAA-equivalent, governed by Operating Circular 5

sample_vendors.name: Amazon Web Services
sample_vendors.service: Hosting for digital banking (online/mobile), Zelle integration, fraud analytics workloads
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: FFIEC Outsourced Cloud Computing statement guidance

sample_vendors.name: Microsoft Azure Active Directory
sample_vendors.service: Employee and customer identity provider for digital channels

sample_vendors.name: Datadog
sample_vendors.service: APM, log aggregation, audit log forwarding target, SIEM-adjacent

sample_vendors.name: Vanta
sample_vendors.service: Compliance evidence collection, FFIEC CAT and SOX evidence automation
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: Internal efficiency tool, no direct customer data

sample_vendors.name: Pershing (BNY Mellon subsidiary)
sample_vendors.service: Correspondent clearing, custody, trade execution for wealth management customers
sample_vendors.regulatory_dependency: SEC/FINRA-supervised introducing broker-dealer, correspondent bank regulatory expectations

ropa.purpose: Records of Processing Activities under GLBA Safeguards Rule, state data breach notification laws (NC, SC, VA, TN), CFPB Regulation P, and FFIEC audit requirements

categories.name: Customer onboarding and account management
processing_activities: Customer onboarding and due diligence (CIP, KYC, beneficial ownership)
Deposit account management (DDA, savings, CDs, MMA, overdraft decisions)
Loan origination and servicing (consumer and commercial)
Credit reporting (furnishing to bureaus, FCRA-compliant disputes)

categories.name: Payments and transfers
processing_activities: Wire transfers (Fedwire, correspondent wires, outbound international)
ACH origination and returns (FedACH, same-day ACH)
Zelle and person-to-person payments (Early Warning network)
ATM transactions (own fleet and surcharge-free network)

categories.name: Card services
processing_activities: Card transactions (debit, credit, ATM, dispute workflow)

categories.name: BSA / AML / Sanctions
processing_activities: AML transaction monitoring (BSA Officer, suspicious activity report workflow)

categories.name: Digital channels
processing_activities: Mobile and online banking (session, MFA, device fingerprint)

categories.name: Internal access and HR
processing_activities: Employee access to customer NPI (need-to-know, monitored)

tier_1_critical: FIS Profile core banking (Tier 1, RTO 4 hours)
Wire transfer processing via ACI (Tier 1, RTO 2 hours, RPO zero for in-flight wires)
FedLine Advantage access (Tier 1, RTO 1 hour, regulator-mandated)
tier_2_essential: Jack Henry trust operations (Tier 2, RTO 24 hours)
Fiserv card services authorization (Tier 2, RTO 4 hours)

validation_flows.name: Wire fraud incident response (BEC on commercial customer)
OFAC match resolution (potential sanctions hit on wire or account opening)
Third-party vendor breach notification (FIS or Fiserv incident)
Catastrophic BCP activation (Charlotte data center loss, regional event)
GLBA Safeguards exception (control gap with material customer NPI exposure)

audit_log_forwarding.target_siem: Datadog (US region), with secondary archival to Splunk Cloud for FFIEC IT Examination Handbook retention
audit_log_forwarding.retention_rationale: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook and SOX retention (7 years), with hot storage 1 year and cold storage 6 years

incidents_for_demo.title: Business email compromise attempt against commercial loan customer (October 2025)
incidents_for_demo.summary: Spoofed CFO email instructed commercial loan customer (regional construction firm) to redirect a $2.3M wire. Meridian call-back verification caught the request 38 minutes after wire release; recall initiated within 2 hours, $1.8M recovered via correspondent bank and Federal Reserve recall window.
incidents_for_demo.lessons_learned: Lowered high-value wire dollar threshold triggering mandatory call-back from $500K to $250K for first-time beneficiary changes. Required dual approval for any wire where beneficiary bank changed within 14 days. Quarterly BEC tabletop with top 50 commercial customers.
